industry: AI Edge Compute / Rugged Transportable Systems
thesis: OSS is at an inflection point with accelerating revenue (+55-70% YoY), expanding gross margins (51-58%), and a pristine balance sheet ($36M+ liquidity, zero debt). The company is a pure-play on defense AI edge computing with a $400M pipeline and development-to-production conversion catalysts. Small scale and customer concentration keep it speculative, but risk/reward is attractive at ~$350M market cap.

drivers: Defense AI spending accelerates and OSS captures meaningful share of $400M pipeline
Development contracts convert to production at scale in 2025-2026
Gross margins sustain above 50% as mix shifts entirely to AI Transportable

risks: Revenue growth stalls as development programs fail to convert
Customer concentration in defense primes creates single-program risk
Competition from larger defense contractors erodes pricing power

base_case.assumptions: 20-25% revenue growth continues through 2027, gross margins stabilize around 42%, and the company reaches sustained profitability by 2027.

catalysts.event: Q3 2026 earnings — H2 production ramp evidence

key_risks: Small revenue base creates high operating leverage risk
Defense budget cuts or program cancellations
GPU technology obsolescence requiring continuous R&D
